name: Islamic Center of Irvine
mission: Mission statement - To establish a comprehensive center promoting Islamic values and reflecting excellence in serving the needs of the community
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2024 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: MUHSEN-Certified Organization (MUHSEN (Muslims Understanding & Helping Special Education Needs))

zakatClaimEvidence: ICOI's Community Assistance Program is dedicated to supporting those in need through a variety of essential services, including food distribution, clothing, tuition aid, and financial assistance. Your generosity helps us continue this vital work. If you'd like to make a difference and support our community, please click the button below to donate. Click here to donate Zakat Ul Maal. ... If you would like to contribute funds to help needy families you can do so by either donating your Zakat ul-Maal or your Sadaqah. ... 100% of the Islamic Center Of Irvine's Zakat fund goes towards providing zakat eligible members in the community. ICOI's fund helps needy individuals by providing a rent and utilities assistance program, food and other basic needs, assistance for stranded travelers, as well as covering the costs of mental health services and other healthcare for needy individuals. (Source: https://www.icoi.net/community-assistance/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 26 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: No outcome measurement or metrics tracking

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 21/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, religious cultural cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate overall score due to its excellent financial efficiency and strong liquidity, though its total score is tempered by a lack of external impact verification.

Increase transparency regarding third-party program evaluations

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium alignment for Muslim donors by providing essential religious and cultural services [2]. Its status as a ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE institution confirms its adherence to specific Islamic charitable requirements [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The center demonstrates high financial health with a program expense ratio of 89.9% and holds 26.3 months of working capital [1][3]. While its capacity-building efforts are significant, the lack of cost-per-beneficiary data limits a full assessment of program effectiveness [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc serves as a comprehensive community hub dedicated to promoting Islamic values and excellence in service.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 89.9% ensures the vast majority of funds reach community services
Exceptional fundraising efficiency with $0.00 spent per $1 raised
Strong liquidity position with 26.3 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc operates as a religious and community center providing spiritual and social services to the Irvine area [1]. In the 2022 fiscal year, the organization reported total revenue of $2,209,994 to support its mission of serving community needs [3]. The center is recognized as ZAKAT-ELIGIBLE, maintaining a dedicated community assistance program to distribute funds to those in need [2].

case_against.mitigation_notes: The high program ratio suggests funds are being used for their intended purpose despite the lack of granular impact reporting.
case_against.summary: The primary limitations include <cite id="13">missing beneficiary data</cite> and the absence of a formal theory of change.

confidence.notes: Strong financial data but limited impact reporting.

alignment.explanation: The center is a strong fit for local donors, offering <cite id="4">verified zakat eligibility</cite> across multiple asnaf categories.
alignment.improvement: Alignment could be improved by <cite id="15">clarifying the specific zakat allocation policy</cite> and eligible use categories for prospective donors.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust the organization due to its <cite id="9">fully independent board</cite> and <cite id="12">Candid Bronze Seal</cite> status.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the charity could <cite id="14">document a clear theory of change</cite> with an explicit causal pathway for its community interventions.

impact.explanation: Operations are efficient with an <cite id="5">89.9% program expense ratio</cite> and <cite id="8">high working capital</cite> of 24 months.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="13">consistently publishing beneficiary counts</cite> so cost-per-beneficiary can be accurately assessed.

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Supports local religious and social service institutions to strengthen community infrastructure.

rich_narrative.headline: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc serves as a comprehensive community hub dedicated to promoting Islamic values and excellence in service.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize supporting local Islamic community infrastructure and want to fulfill zakat through a Southern California-based institution with strong financial reserves.
donor_motivations: Want to support a MUHSEN-certified, inclusive mosque environment
Value high financial efficiency and zero fundraising costs
Prefer supporting established local centers with significant grantmaking reach
Seek to fulfill zakat obligations through verified local asnaf categories
giving_considerations: The organization focuses on local community needs rather than international relief
Financial reserves are high, suggesting long-term stability rather than immediate funding urgency
Detailed impact data (e.g., number of individuals served) is not yet fully transparent
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-volume international humanitarian aid or those who require rigorous third-party RCT evaluations of program impact.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects verified outcomes and high program ratios, but lacks public disclosure of specific beneficiary counts or external evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The organization creates impact by providing a physical and spiritual space for worship, education, and social support, while acting as a fiscal sponsor and grantmaker for local charitable initiatives.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that the $2.2M in annual revenue is effectively translating into improved community wellbeing.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While the center has strong reserves, its $2.2M revenue and grantmaking activity show capacity to absorb more for community expansion.

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by its <cite id="7">significant grantmaking capacity</cite> and <cite id="11">MUHSEN certification</cite>.

strengths.detail: The organization allocates <cite id="5">89.9% of its total expenses</cite> directly to its core community and religious programs.
ICOI maintains a <cite id="6">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, meaning donor contributions are not diluted by solicitation costs.
The center maintains <cite id="8">26.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant financial stability and long-term operational security.

strengths_deep_dive: The Islamic Center of Irvine demonstrates a strong commitment to financial transparency and governance, evidenced by its <cite id="9">100% independent board of directors</cite>. This governance structure supports a massive grantmaking operation, where the center distributed <cite id="10">over $2.1 million in domestic grants</cite> in a single tax year. Furthermore, the organization has achieved <cite id="11">MUHSEN certification</cite>, highlighting its dedication to disability inclusion and accessibility within the Muslim community. These operational strengths are paired with a <cite id="12">Bronze Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, indicating a baseline commitment to public reporting and accountability.
rich_narrative.summary: The Islamic Center of Irvine Inc (ICOI) serves as a vital community hub in Southern California, dedicated to <cite id="1">promoting Islamic values and excellence in service</cite>. Operating as a religious and social services provider, the organization reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $2,209,994 in the 2022 fiscal year</cite>, reflecting a steady <cite id="3">three-year revenue growth rate of 10.6%</cite>. ICOI distinguishes itself through a dual focus on spiritual development and direct community support, maintaining a <cite id="4">zakat-eligible community assistance program</cite> that serves local families in financial distress. The organization demonstrates high financial efficiency, directing <cite id="5">89.9% of its total expenses toward program services</cite>. Notably, the center operates with <cite id="6">exceptional fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending $0.00 to raise every $1 in donations, which ensures that contributor funds are maximized for community impact rather than administrative overhead. Beyond local services, ICOI acts as a significant grantmaker, distributing <cite id="7">$2,125,949 in domestic grants</cite> to partner organizations like the Rahma Center to expand its reach and service capacity.
